teamObjective.explanation: Given a weak 4-card hand with only small non-trump spades and no visible pairs/bombs, the best team play is to lead the lowest card. This minimizes resource loss and maximizes the chance partner can assume control cheaply.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner has not acted yet and holds 3 cards (public hand count). Without asserting specific cards, partner is likely to hold a mix of ranks around/above 7 plus possible trumps. By leading a low non-trump single, I signal weakness in the lead suit and give partner a cheap opportunity to take the trick with a moderate high card or trump, preserving their stronger combinations.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents each hold 3 cards and are likely to contain higher non-trump cards and trumps. They will try to win the trick cheaply if possible. Leading a very low card forces them to spend a real high card or a trump if they want control; leading higher lets them win with a slightly lower card.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Given a weak 4-card hand with only small non-trump spades and no visible pairs/bombs, the best team play is to lead the lowest card. This minimizes resource loss and maximizes the chance partner can assume control cheaply.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Leads my highest non-trump. This maximizes the chance I win the trick outright against weak opponent hands, but with only ranks 3-6 and trump rank 7, any 7+ non-trump or any trump beats it. It likely wastes my best single and leaves me with even weaker follow-ups.

risks: Schema repair may preserve imperfect reasoning from the raw model output.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Treat the repaired trace as schema-normalized reasoning; do not add hidden-card facts beyond the raw output.

notes: Deterministically schema-repaired from a ToM-prompted raw output; selectedActionId is unchanged.
